hypothesis_en: Madam President, Mr Barroso, I would first of all like to congratulate Mr Barroso on the content of this afternoon's communication and I would like to express my satisfaction that he is the chosen candidate to chair the European Commission. There are at least three reasons for this singular situation: firstly, because of enlargement. I do not know if we realize that this is the first time that we are going to have a Commission after enlargement. Secondly, because five years ago there had been no terrible terrorist attacks on 11 September or 11 March in Madrid. And thirdly, also because these events have been accompanied by Perhaps for these three reasons, in colloquial terms, we should make a stop on the way, but in politics there are no stops on the way and in the European Union there can be no stops or stops. I think that today, in the afternoon, there is a conclusion on which we all agree, we all said that we must strengthen the European institutions and we all said with special emphasis that we must strengthen the Commission. And you have also said very well that it is not so much about having many powers in the Commission as it is about knowing how to lead, direct, order, set priorities, and I agree with two of the keys you mentioned this afternoon: firstly, that the Commissioners must know how to lead, direct, order the work of the ministers of the nations that make up the European Union, and secondly, that there will be no I have no doubt that the strengthening of the Commission requires that the President of the Commission have a I would like to ask you, Mr President, to give your support to the first and broadest support in this House, and for this reason, when we vote on the appointment of this candidate tomorrow, I would ask that, beyond personal considerations and ideological issues, we think about institutional stability.
reference_en: Madam President Mr Durão Barroso first of all let me congratulate you on the content of your speech this afternoon and let me express my satisfaction (1.35) at the fact that you have been designated to lead the European Commission now you said quite rightly this is not these are not normal times a= and this is not a normal Commission that I that you are to lead in the next few months there are at least three reasons why this situation is peculiar first of all enlargement I'm not sure we realise that this is the first time that we're going to have a Commission the first Commission after enlargement (1.14) secondly because five years ago the terrible terrorist attacks of the eleventh of September and the eleventh of March in Madrid had not happened and thirdly because (1.31) these things were accompanied by increased abstention in the European elections it may be for those three reasons that (1.8) we can say that perhaps we need a a breathing space but there's there are no breathing spaces in politics and there is no way in the European Union that you can stop and take stock we need certainly however to think think more deeply than ever ~a_nd at the same time aim higher than ever in the past when we are thinking about the future of the European Union (1.24) I think there's a conclusion that we can all agree on this afternoon we've all said that we need to strengthen the European institutions and we've all particularly stressed that the Commission needs to be strengthened that's something that everybody agrees on the question is how to strengthen the Commission further and you said it very well it's not a question of getting lots of powers in the Commission it's a matter of leadership of management of setting priorities (1.08) and I'm going to remember two of the things that you mentioned first of all Commissioners must above all know how to manage how to lead the work of the ministers of the nations that make up the European Union and secondly you said that there will not be an external policy of the European Union until there is a solid serious internal domestic policy that can deal with the common problems that we all have in Europe so tomorrow's question is how to strengthen the Commission shall we strengthen the President of the Commission or shall we punish him through our vote oh I have no doubt in my mind that a stronger Commission means a President of the Commission with broad with deep support in this House so tomorrow when we vote for the designation of this candidate I would say for the confirmation of this candidate I would appeal over and above ideology for a vote for institutional stability
source_es: Señora Presidente Señor Durão r= Barroso quiero en primer lugar felicitar al señor Durão Barroso por el contenido de la comunicación de esta tarde y quiero expresar mi satisfacción por el hecho de que él sea el candidato escogido para presidir la Comisión Europea lo ha dicho usted muy bien no es cualquier momento no vivimos cualquier circunstancia no es cualquier Comisión la que usted va a presidir en los próximos meses y por lo menos hay tres razones que confirman esta situación tan singular primero por la ampliación no sé si nos damos cuenta que es la primera vez que vamos a tener una Comisión tras la ampliación en segundo lugar porque hace cinco años no se habían producido los terribles atentados terroristas del once de septiembre ni del once de marzo en Madrid y en tercer lugar también porque estos hechos han venido acompañados por ~una_ un aumento en la abstención en las elecciones europeas por es= posiblemente por ~estros tres razones e= en términos coloquiales tendríamos que hacer un alto en el camino pero en la política no hay altos en el camino y en la Unión Europea no se puede hacer ningún alto ni ninguna parada pero ese alto en el camino debe ser sustituido por una reflexión más profunda que nunca y que al mismo tiempo nos permita levantar la mirada más alta que nunca a la hora de abordar el futuro de la Unión Europea en la tarde de hoy yo creo que hay una conclusión en la que todos estamos de acuerdo todos hemos dicho que hay que fortalecer las instituciones europeas y todos con especial énfasis hemos dicho que hay que fortalecer la Comisión en ese punto estamos todos de acuerdo la cuestión es cómo podemos fortalecer más la Comisión y usted también lo ha dicho muy bien no es tanto tener muchas competencias en la Comisión es cuestión de saber liderar dirigir ordenar marcar prioridades y me quedo con dos de las claves que usted ha señalado en la tarde de hoy la primera que los Comisarios tienen que sobre todo saber liderar dirigir ordenar el trabajo de los ministros de las naciones que componen la Unión Europea y en segundo lugar que no habrá una política exterior para la Unión Europea si antes no hay una política interior sólida en la ~Unón Europea capaz de afrontar problemas comunes que tenemos todos los europeos y por eso la pregunta para mañana es cómo fortalecemos la Comusió= la Comisión fortaleciendo al Presidente de la Comisión o castigando al voto con el voto al Presidente de la Comisión yo no tengo ninguna duda que el fortalecimiento de la Comisión exige que ~yel Presidente de la Comisión tenga un primer y amplio respaldo en el conjunto de esta Cámara y por esta razón cuando mañana votemos en la designación de este candidato yo pediría que por encima de las consideraciones personales y al margen de las cuestiones ideológicas pensemos en la estabilidad institucional muchas gracias
